location: Found inside a treasure chest in a troll-drawn carriage in the southeast of Consecrated Snowfield, a hidden late-game snowfield reached via the Haligtree Secret Medallion. From the Consecrated Snowfield grace, head northeast toward a row of three large rocks visible on the map — the carriage sits near the second rock. Resting at a grace before heading out helps ensure the wandering carriage has spawned.
tactics: Reach Consecrated Snowfield via the Haligtree Secret Medallion (two halves needed) to unlock the shortcut from the Grand Lift of Rold
From Consecrated Snowfield grace, aim northeast for the second of three large rocks in a row shown on the map — the carriage is nearby
Don't confuse this with Ordina, Liturgical Town, a separate location in the same region reachable with Sentry's Torch
Along with the Sword of St. Trina, this is one of only two reusable ways to inflict Sleep — very effective against Godskin Apostles/Nobles, who are weak to it
lore: A candle-holder torch carved with an unusually adult likeness of St. Trina, burning with a lilac flame that carries her narcotic touch — waving it sends out a drowsy purple mist that lulls enemies to sleep.

builds.name: Faith/Dexterity Sleep-status build
builds.why: Faith and Dexterity scaling plus a low-cost Sleep-inflicting skill (Fires of Slumber) make it one of the two reusable Sleep-proc weapons in the game, strong against Godskin enemies.

builds.name: Status-stacking hybrid
builds.why: Pairs well with other Sleep or Madness tools for players building around status ailments rather than raw damage.
